summary: Rhododendron thomsonii (半圆叶杜鹃) is a rhododendron species native to northeastern India, Bhutan, Nepal, Sikkim, southern Tibet, and northern Myanmar, where it grows at elevations of 3,000–4,000 m (9,800–13,100 ft). It is a shrub that grows to 2–4 m in height, with leathery leaves that are oblong-elliptic to ovate or orbicular to obovate, 3–7 by 2–6 cm in size. Flowers are red.
